editorial_reasoning.calendar_tier: TIER 1 — NBA Play-In Tournament final night plus NFL pre-draft week
editorial_reasoning.dominant_topic: NBA playoffs dominate — Tier 1 confirmed by volume and calendar significance

candidates_considered.hook: $50M/year makes him highest-paid non-QB ever — QB money territory
Dynasty officially over? Kerr's 'expiration date' quote plus Curry/Dray emotional moment
Former Rockets star torches Warriors in play-in
Ravens player calls out former coach

stories.headline: Will Anderson Jr. Signs Historic $150M Deal, Highest-Paid Non-QB Ever
stories.editorial_direction: This is massive — $50M/year puts him in QB money territory. Focus on the financial absurdity and what it means for C.J. Stroud's upcoming extension. Play up the 'paying a pass rusher like a quarterback' angle.

tweets.text: Source: Will Anderson is signing a three-year $150 million extension that makes him the highest paid non-quarterback in NFL history. The deal includes $134 million guaranteed.

Will Anderson Jr. in three NFL seasons:

🏈Defensive Rookie of the Year 
🏈2x Pro Bowler
🏈First-Team All-Pro
🏈30 sacks
🏈4 forced fumbles
🏈3 fumble recoveries
🏈64 QB hits

And now: the NFL's first-ever $50M-per-year non-QB.

Highest paid non-quarterbacks in the NFL:

🏈Will Anderson Jr.: $50M
🏈Micah Parsons: $46.5M
🏈Aidan Hutchinson: $45M
🏈Jaxon Smith-Njigba: $42.15M
🏈TJ Watt: $41M
🏈Ja'Marr Chase: $40.25M

The Texans made the 3rd pick in the 2022 and 2023 NFL drafts.

2022: CB Derek Stingley, Jr. who signed a $30M/year extension last year, largest ever for a DB

2023: DE Will Anderson, Jr. who just signed the largest non-QB contract ever at $50M/year

key_stats: $50M/year makes Anderson highest-paid non-QB in NFL history
$134M guaranteed — more than most QB deals
Anderson has 30 sacks in 3 seasons, DPOY runner-up in 2025
Only 24 years old, deal runs through 2029
financial_absurdity: $50M puts him in QB salary territory — only 9 QBs make $50M+
More guaranteed money than many franchise quarterbacks get
Texans now paying $50M to their pass rusher while C.J. Stroud on rookie deal
Deal includes no-trade clause — rare for non-QB
historical_context: First non-QB to reach $50M AAV milestone
Surpasses Micah Parsons' $46.5M deal from last year
Shows how elite pass rushers are now valued like QBs
Part of trend of defensive superstars getting QB-level contracts
implications_for_stroud: Creates massive precedent for Stroud extension talks
No way Stroud accepts less than $50M after this deal
Texans may have just made their QB extension $60M+ by paying Anderson $50M
Anderson deal shows Texans philosophy: pay your stars
cross_sport_connections: Like NBA max contracts — elite players get elite money regardless of position
Similar to how top relievers now get starter money in MLB
Pass rushers becoming the NBA big men of NFL — premium position getting premium pay
meme_angles: Defensive ends eating at the adults' table now
Anderson making more than most starting QBs
C.J. Stroud probably calling his agent right now
research_notes.where_it_goes: Sets the table for Stroud extension negotiations — Anderson deal makes Stroud's ask much higher
texans_context: Team went all-in on 2023 draft class — Stroud and Anderson both getting mega deals
Shows commitment to championship window with this core
Texans have now locked up both cornerstones from historic draft

stories.headline: Warriors Dynasty Officially Over? Kerr Says Jobs Have 'Expiration Date'
stories.editorial_direction: This is the end of an era. Focus on Kerr's emotional moment with Curry/Draymond, the 'expiration date' quote, and what this means for the Warriors future. Play up the uncertainty and finality.

tweets.text:
Steve Kerr to Steph and Draymond at the end of the Suns game: 

"I don't know what's gonna happen next, but I love you guys to death."

Steve Kerr when asked about the emotional moment at the end of the game with Draymond Green and Steph Curry: 

"None of your business"

(h/t @ohnohedidnt24)

Steve Kerr acknowledged after Friday's elimination his time with the Warriors could be done. He will take some time before a decision with management.

"These jobs all have an expiration date."

ESPN story

Scott Foster EJECTED Draymond Green and Devin Booker 

Draymond trolling Suns fans on his way to the locker room 😂😂

emotional_context: Kerr embraced Curry and Draymond in final moments, appeared to say 'thank you'
When asked what he said, Kerr replied 'None of your business' with a smile
Fourth time in seven seasons Warriors have missed playoffs
Dynasty core is aging — Curry 36, Draymond 35
kerr_quotes: 'These jobs all have an expiration date'
'It might still go on. It may not'
'When the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas'
Says he'll take a week or two to decide his future
warriors_decline: 37-45 record this season — worst in Kerr era
Core players dealing with injuries all season
From dynasty to fringe play-in team
player_reactions: Curry: 'I want Coach to be happy... whatever that means for him'
Draymond: 'I don't deal with change well. I don't love it'
Both players clearly want Kerr back but understand the situation
contract_situation: Intentionally avoided extension talks during season
Says he won't coach elsewhere — Warriors or retirement
Lacob will likely offer top money to retain him
what_this_means: Potential end of greatest NBA dynasty since Lakers/Celtics
Curry's championship window may be closing
Warriors face massive roster decisions this offseason
Could be last hurrah for Splash Brothers era

stories.headline: Jalen Green Torches Warriors for 36 Points, Sends Suns to Playoffs
stories.editorial_direction: Former Rockets standout eliminates his former city rivals. Focus on Green's dominant performance and how the Warriors just looked gassed after the Clippers game.

tweets.text:
Guard battle in the Play-In:

Green —                              Curry —
36 PTS                                 17 PTS
6 REB                                    4 REB
8-14 3P                                3-10 3P

Jordan Goodwin vs GSW:

19 PTS
9 REB
6 STL (!!)
7-11 FG
4-7 3P

More points than Steph. More rebounds than Horford. More steals than Draymond. More threes than Steph.

Dillon Brooks on the Suns playing the Thunder in the first round: 

"There are a lot of foul baiters on that team" 😭

green_performance: 8-of-14 from three — tied play-in tournament record
warriors_struggles: Looked gassed from Clippers comeback win
Curry clearly not 100% after knee injury
Only scored 15 points in first quarter — season low
suns_context: Earned 8 seed, will face defending champion Thunder
Jordan Goodwin also dominated with 19-9-6 steals
Team that barely made play-in now in playoffs
Dillon Brooks already talking trash about Thunder
houston_connection: Green was former Rockets #2 overall pick
Now eliminating team from his former city
Phoenix got him in trade after Houston rebuild

stories.headline: Cardinals QB Jacoby Brissett Holds Out for Starter Money
stories.editorial_direction: Veteran backup wants to be paid like a starter. Simple transaction story with some contract context.

tweets.text:
Sources: #AZCardinals QB Jacoby Brissett is not attending Phase 1 of the offseason program and is asking for an extension that pays him as the starter. 

Brissett, set to make $9.06M in '26, has only $1.5M in guaranteed money and wants security. AZ appears willing to address it.

context: Cardinals incumbent starter after offseason moves
Wants to be paid as starter, not backup

stories.headline: Ravens WR Zay Flowers Blames Harbaugh's 'Heavy' Practices for Injuries
stories.editorial_direction: Player calls out former coach's practice methods. Keep it factual, don't editorialize on the controversy.

tweets.text: Ravens wideout Zay Flowers says "heavy" practices under former coach John Harbaugh were the reason Baltimore "had a lot of injuries" last season.

context: Flowers blaming practice intensity for injury issues
Ravens did have significant injury problems in 2025
research_notes.controversy_note: Keep factual — report what Flowers said without taking sides on practice methods

He's the first AL East pitcher with 8+ shutout innings in a start this season.

I can report that a couple hours ago I saw the bears ownership and Kevin Warren at lost marsh golf club in Hammond Indiana conducting some sort of important business meeting with security and suits and every thing. The golf course is directly adjacent to the proposed Indiana property for the Bears. Furthermore I can report that I shot a 97 in sustained winds of 20 mph. You have to report that part if u use my reporting about the secret bears ownership meeting

2-5

He has more 4-RBI games than Mike Trout since he made his debut in 2019.

Absolute legend. His laugh and jokes was the soundtrack of my drives to school with my dad growing up. One of the first sparks that made me say "I think I'd like to try to make people laugh one day". RIP Bob.

closer.hook: Spring training vibes meeting NFL draft chaos

tweets.text: He did say he was going to burn Birmingham to the ground this weekend. We just didn't know he was being literal
